JSON Support

JSON Queries

XQuery patterns for querying JSON documents stored in PhoenixmlDb

#JSON Queries

This guide covers XQuery patterns optimized for querying JSON documents stored in PhoenixmlDb.

#Basic Field Access

#Filtering Documents

#Simple Filters

#Combined Filters

#Null Checks

#Array Queries

#Contains Element

#Array Index Access

#Array Aggregation

#Nested Array Queries

#Nested Object Queries

#Type Handling

#Boolean Fields

#Date Fields

#Joins

#Multiple Joins

#Grouping and Aggregation

#Group By

#Complex Aggregation

#Full-Text Search

#Pagination

#Output as JSON

#XQuery 3.1 Maps

#Serialize to JSON

#Best Practices

  1. Index frequently queried paths — /map/status, /map/customerId

  2. Use typed comparisons — Cast to xs:decimal for numeric comparisons

  3. Avoid // in large collections — Use specific paths

  4. Filter before joining — Reduce join cardinality

  5. Use let for reused expressions — Avoid recomputation

#Next Steps
